'''纒向勝山古墳'''（まきむくかつやまこふん）
*纒向古墳群に属する[[古墳]]。 == 概要 == *所在地：[[桜井市]]東田（ひがいだ）町字勝山 *埋葬者：不明 *築造時期：[[古墳時代]]前期初頭 *墳　形：[[前方後円墳]]（葺石・埴輪無し） *規　模：全長約110m、後円部径約60m、後円部の高さ約7m、周濠幅約25m。 *埋葬部：未調査のため詳細不明、。 *出土遺物：主に周濠部より出土。 **木製の刀剣把手 **団扇 **槽等の祭祀具 **Ｕ字形木製品（クビレ部の周濠から出土） **[[土師器]]（布留０式土器） == [[年輪年代測定]] == *平成13年（[[2001年]]）5月30日。第4次調査において周濠クビレ部埋土中より検出されたヒノキ板材を奈良文化財研究所埋蔵文化財センターにて年輪年代測定の結果、伐採年代は、新しく見積もっても西暦[[210年]]頃と推定されることが判明する。 *年輪年代測定以前は、布留０式土器の出土により土器編年を利用し年代基準を決定していた。土器編年による年代は西暦[[300年]]前後。 == 関連項目 == [[日本の古墳一覧]] {{History-stub}} [[Category:考古学|まきむくかつやまこふん]] [[Category:日本の考古遺跡|まきむくかつやまこふん]] [[Category:古墳|まきむくかつやまこふん]] [[Category:古墳時代|まきむくかつやまこふん]] [[Category:奈良県の歴史|まきむくかつやまこふん]]

'''纒向勝山古墳'''（まきむくかつやまこふん）
*纒向古墳群に属する[[古墳]]。 == 概要 == *所在地：[[桜井市]]東田（ひがいだ）町字勝山 *埋葬者：不明 *築造時期：[[古墳時代]]前期初頭 *墳　形：[[前方後円墳]]（葺石・埴輪無し） *規　模：全長約110m、後円部径約60m、後円部の高さ約7m、周濠幅約25m。 *埋葬部：未調査のため詳細不明、。 *出土遺物：主に周濠部より出土。 **木製の刀剣把手 **団扇 **槽等の祭祀具 **Ｕ字形木製品（クビレ部の周濠から出土） **[[土師器]]（布留０式土器） == [[年輪年代測定]] == *平成13年（[[2001年]]）5月30日。第4次調査において周濠クビレ部埋土中より検出されたヒノキ板材を奈良文化財研究所埋蔵文化財センターにて年輪年代測定の結果、伐採年代は、新しく見積もっても西暦[[210年]]頃と推定されることが判明する。 *年輪年代測定以前は、布留０式土器の出土により土器編年を利用し年代基準を決定していた。土器編年による年代は西暦[[300年]]前後。 == 関連項目 == [[日本の古墳一覧]] {{Japanese-history-stub|まきむくかつやまこふん}} [[Category:考古学|まきむくかつやまこふん]] [[Category:日本の考古遺跡|まきむくかつやまこふん]] [[Category:古墳|まきむくかつやまこふん]] [[Category:古墳時代|まきむくかつやまこふん]] [[Category:奈良県の歴史|まきむくかつやまこふん]]

revision.text:
'''纒向勝山古墳'''（まきむくかつやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]の纒向古墳群に属する[[古墳]]。 == 概要 == *所在地：[[桜井市]]東田（ひがいだ）町字勝山 {{ウィキ座標度分秒|34|32|50.3|N|135|50|05.2|E|}} *埋葬者：不明 *築造時期：[[古墳時代]]前期初頭 *墳 形：[[前方後円墳]]（葺石・埴輪無し） *規 模：全長約110m、後円部径約60m、後円部の高さ約7m、周濠幅約25m。 *埋葬部：未調査のため詳細不明、。 *出土遺物：主に周濠部より出土。 **木製の刀剣把手 **団扇 **槽等の祭祀具 **Ｕ字形木製品（クビレ部の周濠から出土） **[[土師器]]（布留０式土器）
== [[年輪年代測定]] == *平成13年（[[2001年]]）5月30日。第4次調査において周濠クビレ部埋土中より検出されたヒノキ板材を奈良文化財研究所埋蔵文化財センターにて年輪年代測定の結果、伐採年代は、新しく見積もっても西暦[[210年]]頃と推定されることが判明する。 *年輪年代測定以前は、布留０式土器の出土により土器編年を利用し年代基準を決定していた。土器編年による年代は西暦[[300年]]前後。
== 関連項目 == [[日本の古墳一覧]]
==外部リンク== *[http://www.kashikoken.jp/from-site/2000/katuyama4.html 第４次調査 奈良県立橿原考古学研究所] *[http://www.kashikoken.jp/from-site/2001/katuyama4b.html 出土木材年輪年代測定結果 奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]

'''纒向勝山古墳'''（まきむくかつやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]の纒向古墳群に属する[[古墳]]。 == 概要 == *所在地：[[桜井市]]東田（ひがいだ）町字勝山 {{ウィキ座標度分秒|34|32|50.3|N|135|50|05.2|E|}} *埋葬者：不明 *築造時期：[[古墳時代]]前期初頭 *墳 形：[[前方後円墳]]（葺石・埴輪無し） *規 模：全長約110m、後円部径約60m、後円部の高さ約7m、周濠幅約25m。 *埋葬部：未調査のため詳細不明。 *出土遺物：主に周濠部より出土。 **木製の刀剣把手 **団扇 **槽等の祭祀具 **Ｕ字形木製品（クビレ部の周濠から出土） **[[土師器]]（布留０式土器）
== [[年輪年代測定]] == *平成13年（[[2001年]]）5月30日。第4次調査において周濠クビレ部埋土中より検出されたヒノキ板材を奈良文化財研究所埋蔵文化財センターにて年輪年代測定の結果、伐採年代は、新しく見積もっても西暦[[210年]]頃と推定されることが判明する。 *年輪年代測定以前は、布留０式土器の出土により土器編年を利用し年代基準を決定していた。土器編年による年代は西暦[[300年]]前後。 ==埋葬者== 新聞の掲載によると、[[卑弥呼]]の父の墓ではないか？、と言う説があがっている。

[[Image:Makimuku kohungun aerial.jpg|thumb|240px|[[纒向古墳群]]の纒向勝山古墳（上）（{{国土航空写真}}）]] '''纒向勝山古墳'''（まきむくかつやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]の[[纒向古墳群]]に属する[[古墳]]。[[2006年]]1月26日、纒向古墳群の一つとして国の[[史跡]]に指定された。
== 概要 == *所在地：[[桜井市]]東田（ひがいだ）町字勝山 {{ウィキ座標度分秒|34|32|50.3|N|135|50|05.2|E|}} *埋葬者：不明 *築造時期：[[古墳時代]]前期初頭 *墳 形：[[前方後円墳]]（葺石・埴輪無し） *規 模：全長約110m、後円部径約60m、後円部の高さ約7m、周濠幅約25m。 *埋葬部：未調査のため詳細不明。 *出土遺物：主に周濠部より出土。 **木製の刀剣把手 **団扇 **槽等の祭祀具 **Ｕ字形木製品（クビレ部の周濠から出土） **[[土師器]]（布留０式土器）
== [[年輪年代測定]] == *平成13年（[[2001年]]）5月30日。第4次調査において周濠クビレ部埋土中より検出されたヒノキ板材を奈良文化財研究所埋蔵文化財センターにて年輪年代測定の結果、伐採年代は、新しく見積もっても西暦[[210年]]頃と推定されることが判明する。 *年輪年代測定以前は、布留０式土器の出土により土器編年を利用し年代基準を決定していた。土器編年による年代は西暦[[300年]]前後。 ==埋葬者== 新聞の掲載によると、[[卑弥呼]]の父の墓ではないか？、と言う説があがっている{{誰}}。
== 関連項目 == [[日本の古墳一覧]]
==外部リンク== *[http://www.kashikoken.jp/from-site/2000/katuyama4.html 第４次調査 奈良県立橿原考古学研究所] *[http://www.kashikoken.jp/from-site/2001/katuyama4b.html 出土木材年輪年代測定結果 奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]
{{DEFAULTSORT:まきむくかつやまこふん}} {{Japanese-history-stub}}

（{{国土航空写真}}） }} '''纒向勝山古墳'''（まきむくかつやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]の[[纒向古墳群]]に属する[[古墳]]。[[2006年]]1月26日、纒向古墳群の一つとして国の[[史跡]]に指定された。 == 概要 == *所在地：[[桜井市]]東田（ひがいだ）町字勝山 *埋葬者：不明 *築造時期：[[古墳時代]]前期初頭 *墳　形：[[前方後円墳]]（[[葺石]]・[[埴輪]]なし） *規　模：全長約115メートル、後円部径約70メートル、前方部長約45メートル、くびれ部幅26メートル、後円部の高さ約7メートル。周濠幅約25メートル。 *埋葬施設：未調査のため詳細不明。 *出土遺物：主として周濠部より出土。 **木製の刀剣把手 **[[団扇]] **[[槽]]等の祭祀具 **Ｕ字形木製品（くびれ部の周濠から出土） **[[土師器]]（[[布留0式]]土器） == 年輪年代測定 == *[[2001年]]（平成13年）5月30日。第4次調査において周濠くびれ部埋土中より検出されたヒノキ板材を奈良文化財研究所埋蔵文化財センターにて[[年輪年代測定]]の結果、伐採年代は、新しく見積もっても西暦[[210年]]頃と推定されることが判明する。 *年輪年代測定以前は、布留0式土器の出土により土器編年を利用し年代基準を決定していた。土器編年による年代は西暦[[300年]]前後。 ==埋葬者== 新聞の掲載によると、[[卑弥呼]]の父の墓ではないかという説があがっている{{誰2|date=2009年12月}}。 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[纒向古墳群]] ==外部リンク== * {{Cite web |url= http://www.kashikoken.jp/from-site/2000/katuyama4.html |title=桜井市　勝山古墳第４次調査　記者発表資料（2001年3月26日） |publisher=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所 |accessdate=2013-05-04}} * {{Cite web |url= http://www.kashikoken.jp/from-site/2001/katuyama4b.html |title=勝山古墳出土木材年輪年代測定結果について　記者発表資料（2001年5月30日） |publisher=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所 |accessdate=2013-05-04}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:まきむくかつやまこふん}} [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]]

（{{国土航空写真}}） }} '''纒向勝山古墳'''（まきむくかつやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]の[[纒向古墳群]]に属する[[古墳]]。[[2006年]]1月26日、纒向古墳群の一つとして国の[[史跡]]に指定された。 == 概要 == *所在地：[[桜井市]]東田（ひがいだ）町字勝山 *埋葬者：不明 *築造時期：[[古墳時代]]前期初頭 *墳　形：[[前方後円墳]]（[[葺石]]・[[埴輪]]なし） *規　模：全長約115メートル、後円部径約70メートル、前方部長約45メートル、くびれ部幅26メートル、後円部の高さ約7メートル。周濠幅約25メートル。[[File:Katsuyama,makimuku.JPG|thumb|勝山古墳]] *埋葬施設：未調査のため詳細不明。 *出土遺物：主として周濠部より出土。 **木製の刀剣把手 **[[団扇]] **[[槽]]等の祭祀具 **Ｕ字形木製品（くびれ部の周濠から出土） **[[土師器]]（[[布留0式]]土器） == 年輪年代測定 == *[[2001年]]（平成13年）5月30日。第4次調査において周濠くびれ部埋土中より検出されたヒノキ板材を奈良文化財研究所埋蔵文化財センターにて[[年輪年代測定]]の結果、伐採年代は、新しく見積もっても西暦[[210年]]頃と推定されることが判明する。 *年輪年代測定以前は、布留0式土器の出土により土器編年を利用し年代基準を決定していた。土器編年による年代は西暦[[300年]]前後。 ==埋葬者== 新聞の掲載によると、[[卑弥呼]]の父の墓ではないかという説があがっている{{誰2|date=2009年12月}}。 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[纒向古墳群]] ==外部リンク== * {{Cite web |url= http://www.kashikoken.jp/from-site/2000/katuyama4.html |title=桜井市　勝山古墳第４次調査　記者発表資料（2001年3月26日） |publisher=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所 |accessdate=2013-05-04}} * {{Cite web |url= http://www.kashikoken.jp/from-site/2001/katuyama4b.html |title=勝山古墳出土木材年輪年代測定結果について　記者発表資料（2001年5月30日） |publisher=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所 |accessdate=2013-05-04}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:まきむくかつやまこふん}} [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:奈良県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:纒向古墳群]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称= 纒向勝山古墳 |所属=[[纒向古墳群]] |所在地= 奈良県桜井市東田字勝山 | 緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 32|緯度秒 = 50.7 | 経度度 = 135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 5.2 |形状= [[纒向型前方後円墳]] |築造年代= 3世紀前期-後期{{Cite web |url= http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html |title= 纒向遺跡ってどんな遺跡？|publisher=桜井市纒向学研究センター|accessdate=2013-05-04}} |埋葬施設= |規模=全長約115m

{{日本の古墳 |名称= 纒向勝山古墳 |所属=[[纒向古墳群]] |所在地= 奈良県桜井市東田字勝山 | 緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 32|緯度秒 = 50.7 | 経度度 = 135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 5.2 |形状= [[纒向型前方後円墳]] |築造年代= 3世紀前期-後期{{Cite web |url= http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html |title= 纒向遺跡ってどんな遺跡？|publisher=桜井市纒向学研究センター|accessdate=2013-05-04}} |埋葬施設= |規模=全長約115m
後円部径約70m、前方部長約45ｍ、周濠幅約25m |被葬者= 不明 |史跡指定= 国指定（纒向古墳群） |出土品= |特記事項= |画像=[[ファイル:Makimuku kohungun aerial.jpg|250px]] |画像キャプション= [[纒向古墳群]]の纒向勝山古墳（上）
（{{国土航空写真}}） }} '''纒向勝山古墳'''（まきむくかつやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]の[[纒向古墳群]]に属する[[古墳]]。[[2006年]]1月26日、纒向古墳群の一つとして国の[[史跡]]に指定された。 == 概要 == *所在地：[[桜井市]]東田（ひがいだ）町字勝山 *埋葬者：不明 *築造時期：[[古墳時代]]前期初頭 *墳　形：[[前方後円墳]]（[[葺石]]・[[埴輪]]なし） *規　模：全長約115メートル、後円部径約70メートル、前方部長約45メートル、くびれ部幅26メートル、後円部の高さ約7メートル。周濠幅約25メートル。[[File:Katsuyama,makimuku.JPG|thumb|勝山古墳]] *埋葬施設：未調査のため詳細不明。 *出土遺物：主として周濠部より出土。 **木製の刀剣把手 **[[団扇]] **[[槽]]等の祭祀具 **Ｕ字形木製品（くびれ部の周濠から出土） **[[土師器]]（[[布留0式]]土器） == 年輪年代測定 == *[[2001年]]（平成13年）5月30日。第4次調査において周濠くびれ部埋土中より検出されたヒノキ板材を奈良文化財研究所埋蔵文化財センターにて[[年輪年代測定]]の結果、伐採年代は、新しく見積もっても西暦[[210年]]頃と推定されることが判明する。ただしこれは伐採時の年代であり、その後の築造との関連や材の使用・投棄の時期との関連が不明である{{Cite web|url=http://www.makimukugaku.jp/info/iseki.html#minamihizuka |title=纏向遺跡内に所在する主な古墳 -勝山古墳 |publisher=桜井市[[纏向学研究センター]] |accessdate=2018-08-31}}。 *年輪年代測定以前は、周濠跡からの布留0式土器の出土により土器編年を利用し年代基準を決定していた。土器編年による年代は西暦[[300年]]前後。 *同じく周濠跡からは布留0式よりも旧い庄内2式期土器が出土している。土器編年による年代は3世紀前半。ただしこれも周濠跡からの出土のため築造時期の決め手に欠いている。 ==埋葬者== 新聞の掲載によると、[[卑弥呼]]の父の墓ではないかという説があがっている{{誰2|date=2009年12月}}。 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[纒向古墳群]] ==外部リンク== * {{Cite web |url= http://www.kashikoken.jp/from-site/2000/katuyama4.html |title=桜井市　勝山古墳第４次調査　記者発表資料（2001年3月26日） |publisher=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所 |accessdate=2013-05-04}} * {{Cite web |url= http://www.kashikoken.jp/from-site/2001/katuyama4b.html |title=勝山古墳出土木材年輪年代測定結果について　記者発表資料（2001年5月30日） |publisher=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所 |accessdate=2013-05-04}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:まきむくかつやまこふん}} [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:奈良県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:纒向古墳群]] [[Category:日本の墳丘墓]]

（{{国土航空写真}}） }} '''纒向勝山古墳'''（まきむくかつやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]の[[纒向古墳群]]に属する[[古墳]]。[[2006年]]1月26日、纒向古墳群の一つとして国の[[史跡]]に指定された。 == 概要 == *所在地：[[桜井市]]東田（ひがいだ）町字勝山 *埋葬者：不明 *築造時期：[[古墳時代]]前期初頭 *墳　形：[[前方後円墳]]（[[葺石]]・[[埴輪]]なし） *規　模：全長約115メートル、後円部径約70メートル、前方部長約45メートル、くびれ部幅26メートル、後円部の高さ約7メートル。周濠幅約25メートル。[[File:Katsuyama,makimuku.JPG|thumb|勝山古墳]] *埋葬施設：未調査のため詳細不明。 *出土遺物：主として周濠部より出土。 **木製の刀剣把手 **[[団扇]] **[[槽]]等の祭祀具 **Ｕ字形木製品（くびれ部の周濠から出土） **[[土師器]]（[[布留0式]]土器） == 年輪年代測定 == *[[2001年]]（平成13年）5月30日。第4次調査において周濠くびれ部埋土中より検出されたヒノキ板材を奈良文化財研究所埋蔵文化財センターにて[[年輪年代測定]]の結果、伐採年代は、新しく見積もっても西暦[[210年]]頃と推定されることが判明する。ただしこれは伐採時の年代であり、その後の築造との関連や材の使用・投棄の時期との関連が不明である{{Cite web|url=http://www.makimukugaku.jp/info/iseki.html#minamihizuka |title=纏向遺跡内に所在する主な古墳 -勝山古墳 |publisher=桜井市[[纏向学研究センター]] |accessdate=2018-08-31}}。 *年輪年代測定以前は、周濠跡からの布留0式土器の出土により土器編年を利用し年代基準を決定していた。土器編年による年代は西暦[[300年]]前後。 *同じく周濠跡からは布留0式よりも旧い庄内2式期土器が出土している。土器編年による年代は3世紀前半。ただしこれも周濠跡からの出土のため築造時期の決め手に欠いている。 ==埋葬者== 新聞の掲載によると、[[卑弥呼]]の父 → 曾祖父 の墓ではないかという説があがっている{{誰2|date=2009年12月}}。 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[纒向古墳群]] ==外部リンク== * {{Cite web |url= http://www.kashikoken.jp/from-site/2000/katuyama4.html |title=桜井市　勝山古墳第４次調査　記者発表資料（2001年3月26日） |publisher=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所 |accessdate=2013-05-04}} * {{Cite web |url= http://www.kashikoken.jp/from-site/2001/katuyama4b.html |title=勝山古墳出土木材年輪年代測定結果について　記者発表資料（2001年5月30日） |publisher=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所 |accessdate=2013-05-04}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:まきむくかつやまこふん}} [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:奈良県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:纒向古墳群]] [[Category:日本の墳丘墓]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称= 纒向勝山古墳 |所属=[[纒向古墳群]] |所在地= 奈良県桜井市東田字勝山 | 緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 32|緯度秒 = 50.7 | 経度度 = 135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 5.2 |形状= [[纒向型前方後円墳]] |築造年代= 3世紀前期-後期{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.city.sakurai.nara.jp/maki_c/info/iseki.html |title= 纒向遺跡ってどんな遺跡？|publisher=桜井市纒向学研究センター|accessdate=2013-05-04}} |埋葬施設= |規模=全長約115m
後円部径約70m、前方部長約45ｍ、周濠幅約25m |被葬者= 不明 |史跡指定= 国指定（纒向古墳群） |出土品= |特記事項= |画像=[[ファイル:Makimuku kohungun aerial.jpg|250px]] |画像キャプション= [[纒向古墳群]]の纒向勝山古墳（上）
（{{国土航空写真}}） }} '''纒向勝山古墳'''（まきむくかつやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]の[[纒向古墳群]]に属する[[古墳]]。[[2006年]]1月26日、纒向古墳群の一つとして国の[[史跡]]に指定された。 == 概要 == *所在地：[[桜井市]]東田（ひがいだ）町字勝山 *埋葬者：不明 *築造時期：[[古墳時代]]前期初頭 *墳　形：[[前方後円墳]]（[[葺石]]・[[埴輪]]なし） *規　模：全長約115メートル、後円部径約70メートル、前方部長約45メートル、くびれ部幅26メートル、後円部の高さ約7メートル。周濠幅約25メートル。[[File:Katsuyama,makimuku.JPG|thumb|勝山古墳]] *埋葬施設：未調査のため詳細不明。 *出土遺物：主として周濠部より出土。 **木製の刀剣把手 **[[団扇]] **[[槽]]等の祭祀具 **Ｕ字形木製品（くびれ部の周濠から出土） **[[土師器]]（[[布留0式]]土器） == 年輪年代測定 == *[[2001年]]（平成13年）5月30日。第4次調査において周濠くびれ部埋土中より検出されたヒノキ板材を奈良文化財研究所埋蔵文化財センターにて[[年輪年代測定]]の結果、伐採年代は、新しく見積もっても西暦[[210年]]頃と推定されることが判明する。ただしこれは伐採時の年代であり、その後の築造との関連や材の使用・投棄の時期との関連が不明である{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.makimukugaku.jp/info/iseki.html#minamihizuka |title=纏向遺跡内に所在する主な古墳 -勝山古墳 |publisher=桜井市[[纏向学研究センター]] |accessdate=2018-08-31}}。 *年輪年代測定以前は、周濠跡からの布留0式土器の出土により土器編年を利用し年代基準を決定していた。土器編年による年代は西暦[[300年]]前後。 *同じく周濠跡からは布留0式よりも旧い庄内2式期土器が出土している。土器編年による年代は3世紀前半。ただしこれも周濠跡からの出土のため築造時期の決め手に欠いている。 ==埋葬者== 新聞の掲載によると、[[卑弥呼]]の父 → 曾祖父 の墓ではないかという説があがっている{{誰2|date=2009年12月}}。 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[纒向古墳群]] ==外部リンク== * {{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.kashikoken.jp/from-site/2000/katuyama4.html |title=桜井市　勝山古墳第４次調査　記者発表資料（2001年3月26日） |publisher=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所 |accessdate=2013-05-04}} * {{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.kashikoken.jp/from-site/2001/katuyama4b.html |title=勝山古墳出土木材年輪年代測定結果について　記者発表資料（2001年5月30日） |publisher=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所 |accessdate=2013-05-04}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:まきむくかつやまこふん}} [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:奈良県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:纒向古墳群]] [[Category:日本の墳丘墓]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Ancient Japanese tomb}} {{Draft topics|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|geo}}
{{draft}} {{Infobox ancient site| image=File:Makimuku_kohungun_aerial.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E5%8B%9D%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3&params=34_32_50.7_N_135_50_5.2_E_type:landmark_region:JP}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]<ref name=":0">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>.It is a Kofun of [[Makimuku ruins]], and considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later.<ref name=":1">{{Cite book |last=Kidder |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}</ref>{{rp|249}}
The [[Makimuku ruins]], have been very important in providing information for new chronologies of Ancient Japan. [[Hashihaka Kofun]] is the most famous member of the ruins. Two significant burial mounds from this region, namely the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Makimuku Ishizuka|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} and Makimuku Katsuyama, have provided dendrochronological evidence that suggests revisions to established ceramic chronologies.<ref name=":0" />
== Dating == The Kofun is dated to around 200CE, with the nearby {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} being dated to around 180AD.<ref name=":03">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>
It is considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later, so its chrronological position is notable.<ref name=":1" />{{rp|249}}
In the 1990s, a wooden board from the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} was dated to 177 CE with dendrochronology. In 2001, other wood remains from the {{ill|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|ja|纒向勝山古墳}} were dated between 103 and 199 CE. These findings imply construction dates around 180 CE for Makimuku Ishizuka and approximately 200 CE for Makimuku Katsuyama.<ref name=":03" />
== Dendrochronology == {| class="wikitable" !Dates CE !Ceramic Types !Associated Kofun |- |158-188 |Makimuku 1 Shōnai 0 |Ishizuka |- |188-218 |Makimuku 2 Shōnai 1 |Katsuyama |- |218-243 |Makimuku 3 Shōnai 2/3 |Furu 0 |- |243-268 |Makimuku 4 Shōnai 3/5 |Furu 1 Hashihaka |}
==References== {{reflist}}
== Further reading == * [https://heritageofjapan.wordpress.com/yayoi-era-yields-up-rice/the-advent-of-agriculture-and-the-rice-revolution/who-was-queen-himiko/the-yamatai-puzzle-where-was-himikos-headquarters/in-the-news-hashihaka-tomb-may-be-queen-himikos/in-the-news-hokenoyama-tomb-in-nara-queen-himikos-tomb/]

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]][[en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese tomb}} {{Draft topics|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|geo}}
{{Infobox ancient site| image=File:Makimuku_kohungun_aerial.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E5%8B%9D%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3&params=34_32_50.7_N_135_50_5.2_E_type:landmark_region:JP}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]<ref name=":0">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>.It is a Kofun of [[Makimuku ruins]], and considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later.<ref name=":1">{{Cite book |last=Kidder |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}</ref>{{rp|249}}
The [[Makimuku ruins]], have been very important in providing information for new chronologies of Ancient Japan. [[Hashihaka Kofun]] is the most famous member of the ruins. Two significant burial mounds from this region, namely the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Makimuku Ishizuka|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} and Makimuku Katsuyama, have provided dendrochronological evidence that suggests revisions to established ceramic chronologies.<ref name=":0" />
== Dating == The Kofun is dated to around 200CE, with the nearby {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} being dated to around 180AD.<ref name=":03">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>
It is considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later, so its chrronological position is notable.<ref name=":1" />{{rp|249}}
In the 1990s, a wooden board from the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} was dated to 177 CE with dendrochronology. In 2001, other wood remains from the {{ill|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|ja|纒向勝山古墳}} were dated between 103 and 199 CE. These findings imply construction dates around 180 CE for Makimuku Ishizuka and approximately 200 CE for Makimuku Katsuyama.<ref name=":03" />
== Dendrochronology == {| class="wikitable" !Dates CE !Ceramic types !Associated Kofun |- |158-188 |Makimuku 1 Shōnai 0 |Ishizuka |- |188-218 |Makimuku 2 Shōnai 1 |Katsuyama |- |218-243 |Makimuku 3 Shōnai 2/3 |Furu 0 |- |243-268 |Makimuku 4 Shōnai 3/5 |Furu 1 Hashihaka |}
==References== {{reflist}}

In 2001, other wood remains from the {{ill|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|ja|纒向勝山古墳}} were dated between 103 and 199 CE. These findings imply construction dates around 180 CE for Makimuku Ishizuka and approximately 200 CE for Makimuku Katsuyama.<ref name=":03" />
== Dendrochronology == {| class="wikitable" !Dates CE !Ceramic types !Associated Kofun |- |158-188 |Makimuku 1 Shōnai 0 |Ishizuka |- |188-218 |Makimuku 2 Shōnai 1 |Katsuyama |- |218-243 |Makimuku 3 Shōnai 2/3 |Furu 0 |- |243-268 |Makimuku 4 Shōnai 3/5 |Furu 1 Hashihaka |}

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]][[en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese tomb}} {{Draft topics|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|geo}}
{{Infobox ancient site| image=File:Makimuku_kohungun_aerial.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E5%8B%9D%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3&params=34_32_50.7_N_135_50_5.2_E_type:landmark_region:JP}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]<ref name=":0">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>.It is a Kofun of [[Makimuku ruins]], and considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later.<ref name=":1">{{Cite book |last=Kidder |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}</ref>{{rp|249}}
The [[Makimuku ruins]], have been very important in providing information for new chronologies of Ancient Japan. [[Hashihaka Kofun]] is the most famous member of the ruins. Two significant burial mounds from this region, namely the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Makimuku Ishizuka|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} and Makimuku Katsuyama, have provided dendrochronological evidence that suggests revisions to established ceramic chronologies.<ref name=":0" />
== Dating == The Kofun is dated to around 200CE, with the nearby {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} being dated to around 180AD.<ref name=":03">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>
It is considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later, so its chrronological position is notable.<ref name=":1" />{{rp|249}}
In the 1990s, a wooden board from the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} was dated to 177 CE with dendrochronology. In 2001, other wood remains from the {{ill|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|ja|纒向勝山古墳}} were dated between 103 and 199 CE. These findings imply construction dates around 180 CE for Makimuku Ishizuka and approximately 200 CE for Makimuku Katsuyama.<ref name=":03" />
== Dendrochronology == {| class="wikitable" !Dates CE !Ceramic types !Associated Kofun |- |158-188 |Makimuku 1 Shōnai 0 |Ishizuka |- |188-218 |Makimuku 2 Shōnai 1 |Katsuyama |- |218-243 |Makimuku 3 Shōnai 2/3 |Furu 0 |- |243-268 |Makimuku 4 Shōnai 3/5 |Furu 1 Hashihaka |}

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:New pages most likely u]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]][[en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese tomb}} {{Draft topics|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|geo}}
{{Infobox ancient site| image=File:Makimuku_kohungun_aerial.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E5%8B%9D%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3&params=34_32_50.7_N_135_50_5.2_E_type:landmark_region:JP}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]<ref name=":0">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>.It is a Kofun of [[Makimuku ruins]], and considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later.<ref name=":1">{{Cite book |last=Kidder |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}</ref>{{rp|249}}
The [[Makimuku ruins]], have been very important in providing information for new chronologies of Ancient Japan. [[Hashihaka Kofun]] is the most famous member of the ruins. Two significant burial mounds from this region, namely the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Makimuku Ishizuka|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} and Makimuku Katsuyama, have provided dendrochronological evidence that suggests revisions to established ceramic chronologies.<ref name=":0" />
== Dating == The Kofun is dated to around 200CE, with the nearby {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} being dated to around 180AD.<ref name=":03">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>
It is considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later, so its chrronological position is notable.<ref name=":1" />{{rp|249}}
In the 1990s, a wooden board from the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} was dated to 177 CE with dendrochronology. In 2001, other wood remains from the {{ill|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|ja|纒向勝山古墳}} were dated between 103 and 199 CE. These findings imply construction dates around 180 CE for Makimuku Ishizuka and approximately 200 CE for Makimuku Katsuyama.<ref name=":03" />
== Dendrochronology == {| class="wikitable" !Dates CE !Ceramic types !Associated Kofun |- |158-188 |Makimuku 1 Shōnai 0 |Ishizuka |- |188-218 |Makimuku 2 Shōnai 1 |Katsuyama |- |218-243 |Makimuku 3 Shōnai 2/3 |Furu 0 |- |243-268 |Makimuku 4 Shōnai 3/5 |Furu 1 Hashihaka |}
==References== {{reflist}}
== Further reading == * [https://heritageofjapan.wordpress.com/yayoi-era-yields-up-rice/the-advent-of-agriculture-and-the-rice-revolution/who-was-queen-himiko/the-yamatai-puzzle-where-was-himikos-headquarters/in-the-news-hashihaka-tomb-may-be-queen-himikos/in-the-news-hokenoyama-tomb-in-nara-queen-himikos-tomb/]

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Bumping to try to fix the sexagenary stuff]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]][[en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese tomb}} {{Draft topics|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|geo}}
{{Infobox ancient site| image=File:Makimuku_kohungun_aerial.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E5%8B%9D%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3&params=34_32_50.7_N_135_50_5.2_E_type:landmark_region:JP}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]<ref name=":0">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>.It is a Kofun of [[Makimuku ruins]], and considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later.<ref name=":1">{{Cite book |last=Kidder |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}</ref>{{rp|249}}
The [[Makimuku ruins]], have been very important in providing information for new chronologies of Ancient Japan. [[Hashihaka Kofun]] is the most famous member of the ruins. Two significant burial mounds from this region, namely the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Makimuku Ishizuka|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} and Makimuku Katsuyama, have provided dendrochronological evidence that suggests revisions to established ceramic chronologies.<ref name=":0" />
== Dating == The Kofun is dated to around 200CE, with the nearby {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} being dated to around 180AD.<ref name=":03">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>
It is considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later, so its chrronological position is notable.<ref name=":1" />{{rp|249}}
In the 1990s, a wooden board from the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} was dated to 177 CE with dendrochronology. In 2001, other wood remains from the {{ill|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|ja|纒向勝山古墳}} were dated between 103 and 199 CE. These findings imply construction dates around 180 CE for Makimuku Ishizuka and approximately 200 CE for Makimuku Katsuyama.<ref name=":03" />
== Dendrochronology == {| class="wikitable" !Dates CE !Ceramic types !Associated Kofun |- |158-188 |Makimuku 1 Shōnai 0 |Ishizuka |- |188-218 |Makimuku 2 Shōnai 1 |Katsuyama |- |218-243 |Makimuku 3 Shōnai 2/3 |Furu 0 |- |243-268 |Makimuku 4 Shōnai 3/5 |Furu 1 Hashihaka |}
==References== {{reflist}}
== Further reading == * [https://heritageofjapan.wordpress.com/yayoi-era-yields-up-rice/the-advent-of-agriculture-and-the-rice-revolution/who-was-queen-himiko/the-yamatai-puzzle-where-was-himikos-headquarters/in-the-news-hashihaka-tomb-may-be-queen-himikos/in-the-news-hokenoyama-tomb-in-nara-queen-himikos-tomb/]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Completion tier 1]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]][[en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese tomb}} {{Draft topics|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|geo}}
{{Infobox ancient site| image=File:Makimuku_kohungun_aerial.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E5%8B%9D%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3&params=34_32_50.7_N_135_50_5.2_E_type:landmark_region:JP}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]<ref name=":0">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>.It is a Kofun of [[Makimuku ruins]], and considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later.<ref name=":1">{{Cite book |last=Kidder |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}</ref>{{rp|249}}
The [[Makimuku ruins]], have been very important in providing information for new chronologies of Ancient Japan. [[Hashihaka Kofun]] is the most famous member of the ruins. Two significant burial mounds from this region, namely the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Makimuku Ishizuka|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} and Makimuku Katsuyama, have provided dendrochronological evidence that suggests revisions to established ceramic chronologies.<ref name=":0" />
== Dating == The Kofun is dated to around 200CE, with the nearby {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} being dated to around 180AD.<ref name=":03">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>
It is considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later, so its chrronological position is notable.<ref name=":1" />{{rp|249}}
In the 1990s, a wooden board from the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} was dated to 177 CE with dendrochronology. In 2001, other wood remains from the {{ill|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|ja|纒向勝山古墳}} were dated between 103 and 199 CE. These findings imply construction dates around 180 CE for Makimuku Ishizuka and approximately 200 CE for Makimuku Katsuyama.<ref name=":03" />
== Dendrochronology == {| class="wikitable" !Dates CE !Ceramic types !Associated Kofun |- |158-188 |Makimuku 1 Shōnai 0 |Ishizuka |- |188-218 |Makimuku 2 Shōnai 1 |Katsuyama |- |218-243 |Makimuku 3 Shōnai 2/3 |Furu 0 |- |243-268 |Makimuku 4 Shōnai 3/5 |Furu 1 Hashihaka |}
==References== {{reflist}}
== Further reading == * [https://heritageofjapan.wordpress.com/yayoi-era-yields-up-rice/the-advent-of-agriculture-and-the-rice-revolution/who-was-queen-himiko/the-yamatai-puzzle-where-was-himikos-headquarters/in-the-news-hashihaka-tomb-may-be-queen-himikos/in-the-news-hokenoyama-tomb-in-nara-queen-himikos-tomb/]

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]][[en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese tomb}} {{Draft topics|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|geo}}
{{Infobox ancient site| image=File:Makimuku_kohungun_aerial.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E5%8B%9D%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3&params=34_32_50.7_N_135_50_5.2_E_type:landmark_region:JP}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]<ref name=":0">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>.It is a Kofun of [[Makimuku ruins]], and considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later.<ref name=":1">{{Cite book |last=Kidder |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}</ref>{{rp|249}}
The [[Makimuku ruins]], have been very important in providing information for new chronologies of Ancient Japan. [[Hashihaka Kofun]] is the most famous member of the ruins. Two significant burial mounds from this region, namely the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Makimuku Ishizuka|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} and Makimuku Katsuyama, have provided dendrochronological evidence that suggests revisions to established ceramic chronologies.<ref name=":0" />
== Dating == The Kofun is dated to around 200CE, with the nearby {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} being dated to around 180AD.<ref name=":03">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>
It is considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later, so its chrronological position is notable.<ref name=":1" />{{rp|249}}
In the 1990s, a wooden board from the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} was dated to 177 CE with dendrochronology. In 2001, other wood remains from the {{ill|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|ja|纒向勝山古墳}} were dated between 103 and 199 CE. These findings imply construction dates around 180 CE for Makimuku Ishizuka and approximately 200 CE for Makimuku Katsuyama.<ref name=":03" />
== Dendrochronology == {| class="wikitable" !Dates CE !Ceramic types !Associated Kofun |- |158-188 |Makimuku 1 Shōnai 0 |Ishizuka |- |188-218 |Makimuku 2 Shōnai 1 |Katsuyama |- |218-243 |Makimuku 3 Shōnai 2/3 |Furu 0 |- |243-268 |Makimuku 4 Shōnai 3/5 |Furu 1 Hashihaka |}

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]][[en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese tomb}} {{Draft topics|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|geo}}
{{Infobox ancient site| image=File:Makimuku_kohungun_aerial.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E5%8B%9D%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3&params=34_32_50.7_N_135_50_5.2_E_type:landmark_region:JP}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]<ref name=":0">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>.It is a Kofun of [[Makimuku ruins]], and considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later.<ref name=":1">{{Cite book |last=Kidder |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}</ref>{{rp|249}}
The [[Makimuku ruins]], have been very important in providing information for new chronologies of Ancient Japan. [[Hashihaka Kofun]] is the most famous member of the ruins. Two significant burial mounds from this region, namely the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Makimuku Ishizuka|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} and Makimuku Katsuyama, have provided dendrochronological evidence that suggests revisions to established ceramic chronologies.<ref name=":0" />
== Dating == The Kofun is dated to around 200CE, with the nearby {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} being dated to around 180AD.<ref name=":03">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>
It is considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later, so its chrronological position is notable.<ref name=":1" />{{rp|249}}
In the 1990s, a wooden board from the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} was dated to 177 CE with dendrochronology. In 2001, other wood remains from the {{ill|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|ja|纒向勝山古墳}} were dated between 103 and 199 CE. These findings imply construction dates around 180 CE for Makimuku Ishizuka and approximately 200 CE for Makimuku Katsuyama.<ref name=":03" />
== Dendrochronology == {| class="wikitable" !Dates CE !Ceramic types !Associated Kofun |- |158-188 |Makimuku 1 Shōnai 0 |Ishizuka |- |188-218 |Makimuku 2 Shōnai 1 |Katsuyama |- |218-243 |Makimuku 3 Shōnai 2/3 |Furu 0 |- |243-268 |Makimuku 4 Shōnai 3/5 |Furu 1 Hashihaka |}

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Qq deprecated open]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]][[en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese tomb}} {{Draft topics|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|geo}}
{{Infobox ancient site| image=File:Makimuku_kohungun_aerial.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E5%8B%9D%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3&params=34_32_50.7_N_135_50_5.2_E_type:landmark_region:JP}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]<ref name=":0">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>.It is a Kofun of [[Makimuku ruins]], and considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later.<ref name=":1">{{Cite book |last=Kidder |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}</ref>{{rp|249}}
The [[Makimuku ruins]], have been very important in providing information for new chronologies of Ancient Japan. [[Hashihaka Kofun]] is the most famous member of the ruins. Two significant burial mounds from this region, namely the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Makimuku Ishizuka|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} and Makimuku Katsuyama, have provided dendrochronological evidence that suggests revisions to established ceramic chronologies.<ref name=":0" />
== Dating == The Kofun is dated to around 200CE, with the nearby {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} being dated to around 180AD.<ref name=":03">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>
It is considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later, so its chrronological position is notable.<ref name=":1" />{{rp|249}}
In the 1990s, a wooden board from the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} was dated to 177 CE with dendrochronology. In 2001, other wood remains from the {{ill|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|ja|纒向勝山古墳}} were dated between 103 and 199 CE. These findings imply construction dates around 180 CE for Makimuku Ishizuka and approximately 200 CE for Makimuku Katsuyama.<ref name=":03" />
== Dendrochronology == {| class="wikitable" !Dates CE !Ceramic types !Associated Kofun |- |158-188 |Makimuku 1 Shōnai 0 |Ishizuka |- |188-218 |Makimuku 2 Shōnai 1 |Katsuyama |- |218-243 |Makimuku 3 Shōnai 2/3 |Furu 0 |- |243-268 |Makimuku 4 Shōnai 3/5 |Furu 1 Hashihaka |}
==References== {{reflist}}
== Further reading == * [https://heritageofjapan.wordpress.com/yayoi-era-yields-up-rice/the-advent-of-agriculture-and-the-rice-revolution/who-was-queen-himiko/the-yamatai-puzzle-where-was-himikos-headquarters/in-the-news-hashihaka-tomb-may-be-queen-himikos/in-the-news-hokenoyama-tomb-in-nara-queen-himikos-tomb/]

Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]][[en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese tomb}} {{Draft topics|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|geo}}
{{Infobox ancient site| image=File:Makimuku_kohungun_aerial.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E5%8B%9D%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3&params=34_32_50.7_N_135_50_5.2_E_type:landmark_region:JP}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]<ref name=":0">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>.It is a Kofun of [[Makimuku ruins]], and considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later.<ref name=":1">{{Cite book |last=Kidder |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}</ref>{{rp|249}}
The [[Makimuku ruins]], have been very important in providing information for new chronologies of Ancient Japan. [[Hashihaka Kofun]] is the most famous member of the ruins. Two significant burial mounds from this region, namely the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Makimuku Ishizuka|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} and Makimuku Katsuyama, have provided dendrochronological evidence that suggests revisions to established ceramic chronologies.<ref name=":0" />
== Dating == The Kofun is dated to around 200CE, with the nearby {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} being dated to around 180AD.<ref name=":03">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>
It is considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later, so its chrronological position is notable.<ref name=":1" />{{rp|249}}
In the 1990s, a wooden board from the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} was dated to 177 CE with dendrochronology. In 2001, other wood remains from the {{ill|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|ja|纒向勝山古墳}} were dated between 103 and 199 CE. These findings imply construction dates around 180 CE for Makimuku Ishizuka and approximately 200 CE for Makimuku Katsuyama.<ref name=":03" />
== Dendrochronology == {| class="wikitable" !Dates CE !Ceramic types !Associated Kofun |- |158-188 |Makimuku 1 Shōnai 0 |Ishizuka |- |188-218 |Makimuku 2 Shōnai 1 |Katsuyama |- |218-243 |Makimuku 3 Shōnai 2/3 |Furu 0 |- |243-268 |Makimuku 4 Shōnai 3/5 |Furu 1 Hashihaka |}

Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]][[en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese tomb}} {{Draft topics|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|geo}}
{{Infobox ancient site| image=File:Makimuku_kohungun_aerial.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E5%8B%9D%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3&params=34_32_50.7_N_135_50_5.2_E_type:landmark_region:JP}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]<ref name=":0">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>.It is a Kofun of [[Makimuku ruins]], and considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later.<ref name=":1">{{Cite book |last=Kidder |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}</ref>{{rp|249}}
The [[Makimuku ruins]], have been very important in providing information for new chronologies of Ancient Japan. [[Hashihaka Kofun]] is the most famous member of the ruins. Two significant burial mounds from this region, namely the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Makimuku Ishizuka|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} and Makimuku Katsuyama, have provided dendrochronological evidence that suggests revisions to established ceramic chronologies.<ref name=":0" />
== Dating == The Kofun is dated to around 200CE, with the nearby {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} being dated to around 180AD.<ref name=":03">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>
It is considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later, so its chrronological position is notable.<ref name=":1" />{{rp|249}}
In the 1990s, a wooden board from the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} was dated to 177 CE with dendrochronology. In 2001, other wood remains from the {{ill|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|ja|纒向勝山古墳}} were dated between 103 and 199 CE. These findings imply construction dates around 180 CE for Makimuku Ishizuka and approximately 200 CE for Makimuku Katsuyama.<ref name=":03" />
== Dendrochronology == {| class="wikitable" !Dates CE !Ceramic types !Associated Kofun |- |158-188 |Makimuku 1 Shōnai 0 |Ishizuka |- |188-218 |Makimuku 2 Shōnai 1 |Katsuyama |- |218-243 |Makimuku 3 Shōnai 2/3 |Furu 0 |- |243-268 |Makimuku 4 Shōnai 3/5 |Furu 1 Hashihaka |}

Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]][[en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese tomb}} {{Draft topics|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|geo}}
{{Infobox ancient site| image=File:Makimuku_kohungun_aerial.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E5%8B%9D%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3&params=34_32_50.7_N_135_50_5.2_E_type:landmark_region:JP}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]<ref name=":0">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>.It is a Kofun of [[Makimuku ruins]], and considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later.<ref name=":1">{{Cite book |last=Kidder |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}</ref>{{rp|249}}
The [[Makimuku ruins]], have been very important in providing information for new chronologies of Ancient Japan. [[Hashihaka Kofun]] is the most famous member of the ruins. Two significant burial mounds from this region, namely the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Makimuku Ishizuka|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} and Makimuku Katsuyama, have provided dendrochronological evidence that suggests revisions to established ceramic chronologies.<ref name=":0" />
== Dating == The Kofun is dated to around 200CE, with the nearby {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} being dated to around 180AD.<ref name=":03">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>
It is considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later, so its chrronological position is notable.<ref name=":1" />{{rp|249}}
In the 1990s, a wooden board from the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} was dated to 177 CE with dendrochronology. In 2001, other wood remains from the {{ill|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|ja|纒向勝山古墳}} were dated between 103 and 199 CE. These findings imply construction dates around 180 CE for Makimuku Ishizuka and approximately 200 CE for Makimuku Katsuyama.<ref name=":03" />
== Dendrochronology == {| class="wikitable" !Dates CE !Ceramic types !Associated Kofun |- |158-188 |Makimuku 1 Shōnai 0 |Ishizuka |- |188-218 |Makimuku 2 Shōnai 1 |Katsuyama |- |218-243 |Makimuku 3 Shōnai 2/3 |Furu 0 |- |243-268 |Makimuku 4 Shōnai 3/5 |Furu 1 Hashihaka |}

Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]][[en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese tomb}} {{Draft topics|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|geo}}
{{Infobox ancient site| image=File:Makimuku_kohungun_aerial.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E5%8B%9D%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3&params=34_32_50.7_N_135_50_5.2_E_type:landmark_region:JP}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]<ref name=":0">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>.It is a Kofun of [[Makimuku ruins]], and considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later.<ref name=":1">{{Cite book |last=Kidder |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}</ref>{{rp|249}}
The [[Makimuku ruins]], have been very important in providing information for new chronologies of Ancient Japan. [[Hashihaka Kofun]] is the most famous member of the ruins. Two significant burial mounds from this region, namely the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Makimuku Ishizuka|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} and Makimuku Katsuyama, have provided dendrochronological evidence that suggests revisions to established ceramic chronologies.<ref name=":0" />
== Dating == The Kofun is dated to around 200CE, with the nearby {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} being dated to around 180AD.<ref name=":03">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>
It is considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later, so its chrronological position is notable.<ref name=":1" />{{rp|249}}
In the 1990s, a wooden board from the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} was dated to 177 CE with dendrochronology. In 2001, other wood remains from the {{ill|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|ja|纒向勝山古墳}} were dated between 103 and 199 CE. These findings imply construction dates around 180 CE for Makimuku Ishizuka and approximately 200 CE for Makimuku Katsuyama.<ref name=":03" />
== Dendrochronology == {| class="wikitable" !Dates CE !Ceramic types !Associated Kofun |- |158-188 |Makimuku 1 Shōnai 0 |Ishizuka |- |188-218 |Makimuku 2 Shōnai 1 |Katsuyama |- |218-243 |Makimuku 3 Shōnai 2/3 |Furu 0 |- |243-268 |Makimuku 4 Shōnai 3/5 |Furu 1 Hashihaka |}

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]][[en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese tomb}} {{Draft topics|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|geo}}
{{Infobox ancient site| image=File:Makimuku_kohungun_aerial.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E5%8B%9D%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3&params=34_32_50.7_N_135_50_5.2_E_type:landmark_region:JP}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]<ref name=":0">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>.It is a Kofun of [[Makimuku ruins]], and considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later.<ref name=":1">{{Cite book |last=Kidder |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}</ref>{{rp|249}}
The [[Makimuku ruins]], have been very important in providing information for new chronologies of Ancient Japan. [[Hashihaka Kofun]] is the most famous member of the ruins. Two significant burial mounds from this region, namely the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Makimuku Ishizuka|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} and Makimuku Katsuyama, have provided dendrochronological evidence that suggests revisions to established ceramic chronologies.<ref name=":0" />
== Dating == The Kofun is dated to around 200CE, with the nearby {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} being dated to around 180AD.<ref name=":03">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>
It is considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later, so its chrronological position is notable.<ref name=":1" />{{rp|249}}
In the 1990s, a wooden board from the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} was dated to 177 CE with dendrochronology. In 2001, other wood remains from the {{ill|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|ja|纒向勝山古墳}} were dated between 103 and 199 CE. These findings imply construction dates around 180 CE for Makimuku Ishizuka and approximately 200 CE for Makimuku Katsuyama.<ref name=":03" />
== Dendrochronology == {| class="wikitable" !Dates CE !Ceramic types !Associated Kofun |- |158-188 |Makimuku 1 Shōnai 0 |Ishizuka |- |188-218 |Makimuku 2 Shōnai 1 |Katsuyama |- |218-243 |Makimuku 3 Shōnai 2/3 |Furu 0 |- |243-268 |Makimuku 4 Shōnai 3/5 |Furu 1 Hashihaka |}
==References== {{reflist}}
== Further reading == * [https://heritageofjapan.wordpress.com/yayoi-era-yields-up-rice/the-advent-of-agriculture-and-the-rice-revolution/who-was-queen-himiko/the-yamatai-puzzle-where-was-himikos-headquarters/in-the-news-hashihaka-tomb-may-be-queen-himikos/in-the-news-hokenoyama-tomb-in-nara-queen-himikos-tomb/]

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]][[en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese tomb}} {{Draft topics|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|geo}}
{{Infobox ancient site| image=File:Makimuku_kohungun_aerial.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E5%8B%9D%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3&params=34_32_50.7_N_135_50_5.2_E_type:landmark_region:JP}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]<ref name=":0">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>.It is a Kofun of [[Makimuku ruins]], and considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later.<ref name=":1">{{Cite book |last=Kiqqer |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}</ref>{{rp|249}}
The [[Makimuku ruins]], have been very important in providing information for new chronologies of Ancient Japan. [[Hashihaka Kofun]] is the most famous member of the ruins. Two significant burial mounds from this region, namely the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Makimuku Ishizuka|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} and Makimuku Katsuyama, have provided dendrochronological evidence that suggests revisions to established ceramic chronologies.<ref name=":0" />
== Dating == The Kofun is dated to around 200CE, with the nearby {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} being dated to around 180AD.<ref name=":03">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>
It is considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later, so its chrronological position is notable.<ref name=":1" />{{rp|249}}
In the 1990s, a wooden board from the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} was dated to 177 CE with dendrochronology. In 2001, other wood remains from the {{ill|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|ja|纒向勝山古墳}} were dated between 103 and 199 CE. These findings imply construction dates around 180 CE for Makimuku Ishizuka and approximately 200 CE for Makimuku Katsuyama.<ref name=":03" />
== Dendrochronology == {| class="wikitable" !Dates CE !Ceramic types !Associated Kofun |- |158-188 |Makimuku 1 Shōnai 0 |Ishizuka |- |188-218 |Makimuku 2 Shōnai 1 |Katsuyama |- |218-243 |Makimuku 3 Shōnai 2/3 |Furu 0 |- |243-268 |Makimuku 4 Shōnai 3/5 |Furu 1 Hashihaka |}
==References== {{reflist}}

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]][[en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese tomb}} {{Draft topics|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|geo}}
{{Infobox ancient site| image=File:Makimuku_kohungun_aerial.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E5%8B%9D%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3&params=34_32_50.7_N_135_50_5.2_E_type:landmark_region:JP}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]<ref name=":0">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>.It is a Kofun of [[Makimuku ruins]], and considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later.<ref name=":1">{{Cite book |last=Kiqqer |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}</ref>{{rp|249}}
The [[Makimuku ruins]], have been very important in providing information for new chronologies of Ancient Japan. [[Hashihaka Kofun]] is the most famous member of the ruins. Two significant burial mounds from this region, namely the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Makimuku Ishizuka|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} and Makimuku Katsuyama, have provided dendrochronological evidence that suggests revisions to established ceramic chronologies.<ref name=":0" />
== Dating == The Kofun is dated to around 200CE, with the nearby {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} being dated to around 180AD.<ref name=":03">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>
It is considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later, so its chrronological position is notable.<ref name=":1" />{{rp|249}}
In the 1990s, a wooden board from the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} was dated to 177 CE with dendrochronology. In 2001, other wood remains from the {{ill|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|ja|纒向勝山古墳}} were dated between 103 and 199 CE. These findings imply construction dates around 180 CE for Makimuku Ishizuka and approximately 200 CE for Makimuku Katsuyama.<ref name=":03" />
== Dendrochronology == {| class="wikitable" !Dates CE !Ceramic types !Associated Kofun |- |158-188 |Makimuku 1 Shōnai 0 |Ishizuka |- |188-218 |Makimuku 2 Shōnai 1 |Katsuyama |- |218-243 |Makimuku 3 Shōnai 2/3 |Furu 0 |- |243-268 |Makimuku 4 Shōnai 3/5 |Furu 1 Hashihaka |}
==References== {{reflist}}

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]][[en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese tomb}} {{Draft topics|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|geo}}
{{Infobox ancient site| image=File:Makimuku_kohungun_aerial.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E5%8B%9D%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3&params=34_32_50.7_N_135_50_5.2_E_type:landmark_region:JP}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]<ref name=":0">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>.It is a Kofun of [[Makimuku ruins]], and considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later.<ref name=":1">{{Cite book |last=Kiqqer |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}</ref>{{rp|249}}
The [[Makimuku ruins]], have been very important in providing information for new chronologies of Ancient Japan. [[Hashihaka Kofun]] is the most famous member of the ruins. Two significant burial mounds from this region, namely the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Makimuku Ishizuka|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} and Makimuku Katsuyama, have provided dendrochronological evidence that suggests revisions to established ceramic chronologies.<ref name=":0" />
== Dating == The Kofun is dated to around 200CE, with the nearby {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} being dated to around 180AD.<ref name=":03">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>
It is considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later, so its chrronological position is notable.<ref name=":1" />{{rp|249}}
In the 1990s, a wooden board from the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} was dated to 177 CE with dendrochronology. In 2001, other wood remains from the {{ill|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|ja|纒向勝山古墳}} were dated between 103 and 199 CE. These findings imply construction dates around 180 CE for Makimuku Ishizuka and approximately 200 CE for Makimuku Katsuyama.<ref name=":03" />
== Dendrochronology == {| class="wikitable" !Dates CE !Ceramic types !Associated Kofun |- |158-188 |Makimuku 1 Shōnai 0 |Ishizuka |- |188-218 |Makimuku 2 Shōnai 1 |Katsuyama |- |218-243 |Makimuku 3 Shōnai 2/3 |Furu 0 |- |243-268 |Makimuku 4 Shōnai 3/5 |Furu 1 Hashihaka |}
==References== {{reflist}}

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]][[en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese tomb}} {{Draft topics|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|geo}}
{{Infobox ancient site| image=File:Makimuku_kohungun_aerial.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E5%8B%9D%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3&params=34_32_50.7_N_135_50_5.2_E_type:landmark_region:JP}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]<ref name=":0">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>.It is a Kofun of [[Makimuku ruins]], and considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later.<ref name=":1">{{Cite book |last=Kiqqer |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}</ref>{{rp|249}}
The [[Makimuku ruins]], have been very important in providing information for new chronologies of Ancient Japan. [[Hashihaka Kofun]] is the most famous member of the ruins. Two significant Kofuns from this region, namely the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Makimuku Ishizuka|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} and Makimuku Katsuyama, have provided dendrochronological evidence that suggests revisions to established ceramic chronologies.<ref name=":0" />
== Dating == The Kofun is dated to around 200CE, with the nearby {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} being dated to around 180AD.<ref name=":03">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>
It is considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later, so its chrronological position is notable.<ref name=":1" />{{rp|249}}
In the 1990s, a wooden board from the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} was dated to 177 CE with dendrochronology. In 2001, other wood remains from the {{ill|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|ja|纒向勝山古墳}} were dated between 103 and 199 CE. These findings imply construction dates around 180 CE for Makimuku Ishizuka and approximately 200 CE for Makimuku Katsuyama.<ref name=":03" />
== Dendrochronology == {| class="wikitable" !Dates CE !Ceramic types !Associated Kofun |- |158-188 |Makimuku 1 Shōnai 0 |Ishizuka |- |188-218 |Makimuku 2 Shōnai 1 |Katsuyama |- |218-243 |Makimuku 3 Shōnai 2/3 |Furu 0 |- |243-268 |Makimuku 4 Shōnai 3/5 |Furu 1 Hashihaka |}
==References== {{reflist}}
