revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]][[en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese tomb}} {{Draft topics|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|geo}}
{{Infobox ancient site| image=File:Makimuku_kohungun_aerial.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E5%8B%9D%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3&params=34_32_50.7_N_135_50_5.2_E_type:landmark_region:JP}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]<ref name=":0">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>.It is a Kofun of [[Makimuku ruins]], and considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later.<ref name=":1">{{Cite book |last=Kiqqer |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}</ref>{{rp|249}}
The [[Makimuku ruins]], have been very important in providing information for new chronologies of Ancient Japan. [[Hashihaka Kofun]] is the most famous member of the ruins. Two significant Kofuns from this region, namely the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Makimuku Ishizuka|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} and Makimuku Katsuyama, have provided dendrochronological evidence that suggests revisions to established ceramic chronologies.<ref name=":0" />
== Dating == The Kofun is dated to around 200CE, with the nearby {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} being dated to around 180AD.<ref name=":03">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>
It is considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later, so its chrronological position is notable.<ref name=":1" />{{rp|249}}
In the 1990s, a wooden board from the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} was dated to 177 CE with dendrochronology. In 2001, other wood remains from the {{ill|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|ja|纒向勝山古墳}} were dated between 103 and 199 CE. These findings imply construction dates around 180 CE for Makimuku Ishizuka and approximately 200 CE for Makimuku Katsuyama.<ref name=":03" />
== Dendrochronology == {| class="wikitable" !Dates CE !Ceramic types !Associated Kofun |- |158-188 |Makimuku 1 Shōnai 0 |Ishizuka |- |188-218 |Makimuku 2 Shōnai 1 |Katsuyama |- |218-243 |Makimuku 3 Shōnai 2/3 |Furu 0 |- |243-268 |Makimuku 4 Shōnai 3/5 |Furu 1 Hashihaka |}
==References== {{reflist}}
== Further reading == * [https://heritageofjapan.wordpress.com/yayoi-era-yields-up-rice/the-advent-of-agriculture-and-the-rice-revolution/who-was-queen-himiko/the-yamatai-puzzle-where-was-himikos-headquarters/in-the-news-hashihaka-tomb-may-be-queen-himikos/in-the-news-hokenoyama-tomb-in-nara-queen-himikos-tomb/]

Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]][[en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese tomb}} {{Draft topics|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|geo}}
{{Infobox ancient site| image=File:Makimuku_kohungun_aerial.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E5%8B%9D%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3&params=34_32_50.7_N_135_50_5.2_E_type:landmark_region:JP}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]<ref name=":0">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>.It is a Kofun of [[Makimuku ruins]], and considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later.<ref name=":1">{{Cite book |last=Kiqqer |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}</ref>{{rp|249}}
The [[Makimuku ruins]], have been very important in providing information for new chronologies of Ancient Japan. [[Hashihaka Kofun]] is the most famous member of the ruins. Two significant Kofuns from this region, namely the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Makimuku Ishizuka|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} and Makimuku Katsuyama, have provided dendrochronological evidence that suggests revisions to established ceramic chronologies.<ref name=":0" />
== Dating == The Kofun is dated to around 200CE, with the nearby {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} being dated to around 180AD.<ref name=":03">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>
It is considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later, so its chrronological position is notable.<ref name=":1" />{{rp|249}}
In the 1990s, a wooden board from the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} was dated to 177 CE with dendrochronology. In 2001, other wood remains from the {{ill|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|ja|纒向勝山古墳}} were dated between 103 and 199 CE. These findings imply construction dates around 180 CE for Makimuku Ishizuka and approximately 200 CE for Makimuku Katsuyama.<ref name=":03" />
== Dendrochronology == {| class="wikitable" !Dates CE !Ceramic types !Associated Kofun |- |158-188 |Makimuku 1 Shōnai 0 |Ishizuka |- |188-218 |Makimuku 2 Shōnai 1 |Katsuyama |- |218-243 |Makimuku 3 Shōnai 2/3 |Furu 0 |- |243-268 |Makimuku 4 Shōnai 3/5 |Furu 1 Hashihaka |}

{{Infobox ancient site| image=File:Makimuku_kohungun_aerial.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E5%8B%9D%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3&params=34_32_50.7_N_135_50_5.2_E_type:landmark_region:JP}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]<ref name=":0">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>.It is a Kofun of [[Makimuku ruins]], and considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later.<ref name=":1">{{Cite book |last=Ki11er |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}</ref>{{rp|249}}
The [[Makimuku ruins]], have been very important in providing information for new chronologies of Ancient Japan. [[Hashihaka Kofun]] is the most famous member of the ruins. Two significant Kofuns from this region, namely the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Makimuku Ishizuka|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} and Makimuku Katsuyama, have provided dendrochronological evidence that suggests revisions to established ceramic chronologies.<ref name=":0" />
== Dating == The Kofun is dated to around 200CE, with the nearby {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} being dated to around 180AD.<ref name=":03">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>
It is considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later, so its chrronological position is notable.<ref name=":1" />{{rp|249}}
In the 1990s, a wooden board from the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} was dated to 177 CE with dendrochronology. In 2001, other wood remains from the {{ill|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|ja|纒向勝山古墳}} were dated between 103 and 199 CE. These findings imply construction dates around 180 CE for Makimuku Ishizuka and approximately 200 CE for Makimuku Katsuyama.<ref name=":03" />
== Dendrochronology == {| class="wikitable" !Dates CE !Ceramic types !Associated Kofun |- |158-188 |Makimuku 1 Shōnai 0 |Ishizuka |- |188-218 |Makimuku 2 Shōnai 1 |Katsuyama |- |218-243 |Makimuku 3 Shōnai 2/3 |Furu 0 |- |243-268 |Makimuku 4 Shōnai 3/5 |Furu 1 Hashihaka |}

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| image=File:Makimuku_kohungun_aerial.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E5%8B%9D%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3&params=34_32_50.7_N_135_50_5.2_E_type:landmark_region:JP}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]<ref name=":0">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>.It is a Kofun of [[Makimuku ruins]], and considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later.<ref name=":1">{{Cite book |last=Ki11er |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}</ref>{{rp|249}}
The [[Makimuku ruins]], have been very important in providing information for new chronologies of Ancient Japan. [[Hashihaka Kofun]] is the most famous member of the ruins. Two significant Kofuns from this region, namely the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Makimuku Ishizuka|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} and Makimuku Katsuyama, have provided dendrochronological evidence that suggests revisions to established ceramic chronologies.<ref name=":0" />
== Dating == The Kofun is dated to around 200CE, with the nearby {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} being dated to around 180AD.<ref name=":03">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>
It is considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later, so its chrronological position is notable.<ref name=":1" />{{rp|249}}
In the 1990s, a wooden board from the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} was dated to 177 CE with dendrochronology. In 2001, other wood remains from the {{ill|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|ja|纒向勝山古墳}} were dated between 103 and 199 CE. These findings imply construction dates around 180 CE for Makimuku Ishizuka and approximately 200 CE for Makimuku Katsuyama.<ref name=":03" />
== Dendrochronology == {| class="wikitable" !Dates CE !Ceramic types !Associated Kofun |- |158-188 |Makimuku 1 Shōnai 0 |Ishizuka |- |188-218 |Makimuku 2 Shōnai 1 |Katsuyama |- |218-243 |Makimuku 3 Shōnai 2/3 |Furu 0 |- |243-268 |Makimuku 4 Shōnai 3/5 |Furu 1 Hashihaka |}

Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]][[en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese tomb}} {{Draft topics|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|geo}}
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| image=File:Makimuku_kohungun_aerial.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E5%8B%9D%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3&params=34_32_50.7_N_135_50_5.2_E_type:landmark_region:JP}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]<ref name=":0">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>.It is a Kofun of [[Makimuku ruins]], and considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later.<ref name=":1">{{Cite book |last=Ki11er |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}</ref>{{rp|249}}
The [[Makimuku ruins]], have been very important in providing information for new chronologies of Ancient Japan. [[Hashihaka Kofun]] is the most famous member of the ruins. Two significant Kofuns from this region, namely the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Makimuku Ishizuka|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} and Makimuku Katsuyama, have provided dendrochronological evidence that suggests revisions to established ceramic chronologies.<ref name=":0" />
== Dating == The Kofun is dated to around 200CE, with the nearby {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} being dated to around 180AD.<ref name=":03">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>
It is considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later, so its chrronological position is notable.<ref name=":1" />{{rp|249}}
In the 1990s, a wooden board from the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} was dated to 177 CE with dendrochronology. In 2001, other wood remains from the {{ill|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|ja|纒向勝山古墳}} were dated between 103 and 199 CE. These findings imply construction dates around 180 CE for Makimuku Ishizuka and approximately 200 CE for Makimuku Katsuyama.<ref name=":03" />
== Dendrochronology == {| class="wikitable" !Dates CE !Ceramic types !Associated Kofun |- |158-188 |Makimuku 1 Shōnai 0 |Ishizuka |- |188-218 |Makimuku 2 Shōnai 1 |Katsuyama |- |218-243 |Makimuku 3 Shōnai 2/3 |Furu 0 |- |243-268 |Makimuku 4 Shōnai 3/5 |Furu 1 Hashihaka |}

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Completion tier 1]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]][[en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese tomb}} {{Draft topics|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|geo}}
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| image=File:Makimuku_kohungun_aerial.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E5%8B%9D%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3&params=34_32_50.7_N_135_50_5.2_E_type:landmark_region:JP}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]<ref name=":0">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>.It is a Kofun of [[Makimuku ruins]], and considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later.<ref name=":1">{{Cite book |last=Ki11er |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}</ref>{{rp|249}}
The [[Makimuku ruins]], have been very important in providing information for new chronologies of Ancient Japan. [[Hashihaka Kofun]] is the most famous member of the ruins. Two significant Kofuns from this region, namely the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Makimuku Ishizuka|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} and Makimuku Katsuyama, have provided dendrochronological evidence that suggests revisions to established ceramic chronologies.<ref name=":0" />
== Dating == The Kofun is dated to around 200CE, with the nearby {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} being dated to around 180AD.<ref name=":03">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>
It is considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later, so its chrronological position is notable.<ref name=":1" />{{rp|249}}
In the 1990s, a wooden board from the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} was dated to 177 CE with dendrochronology. In 2001, other wood remains from the {{ill|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|ja|纒向勝山古墳}} were dated between 103 and 199 CE. These findings imply construction dates around 180 CE for Makimuku Ishizuka and approximately 200 CE for Makimuku Katsuyama.<ref name=":03" />
== Dendrochronology == {| class="wikitable" !Dates CE !Ceramic types !Associated Kofun |- |158-188 |Makimuku 1 Shōnai 0 |Ishizuka |- |188-218 |Makimuku 2 Shōnai 1 |Katsuyama |- |218-243 |Makimuku 3 Shōnai 2/3 |Furu 0 |- |243-268 |Makimuku 4 Shōnai 3/5 |Furu 1 Hashihaka |}
==References== {{reflist}}
== Further reading == * [https://heritageofjapan.wordpress.com/yayoi-era-yields-up-rice/the-advent-of-agriculture-and-the-rice-revolution/who-was-queen-himiko/the-yamatai-puzzle-where-was-himikos-headquarters/in-the-news-hashihaka-tomb-may-be-queen-himikos/in-the-news-hokenoyama-tomb-in-nara-queen-himikos-tomb/]

Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]][[en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese tomb}} {{Draft topics|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|geo}}
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| image=File:Makimuku_kohungun_aerial.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E5%8B%9D%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3&params=34_32_50.7_N_135_50_5.2_E_type:landmark_region:JP}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]<ref name=":0">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>.It is a Kofun of [[Makimuku ruins]], and considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later.<ref name=":1">{{Cite book |last=Ki11er |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}</ref>{{rp|249}}
The [[Makimuku ruins]], have been very important in providing information for new chronologies of Ancient Japan. [[Hashihaka Kofun]] is the most famous member of the ruins. Two significant Kofuns from this region, namely the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Makimuku Ishizuka|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} and Makimuku Katsuyama, have provided dendrochronological evidence that suggests revisions to established ceramic chronologies.<ref name=":0" />
== Dating == The Kofun is dated to around 200CE, with the nearby {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} being dated to around 180AD.<ref name=":03">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>
It is considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later, so its chrronological position is notable.<ref name=":1" />{{rp|249}}
In the 1990s, a wooden board from the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} was dated to 177 CE with dendrochronology. In 2001, other wood remains from the {{ill|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|ja|纒向勝山古墳}} were dated between 103 and 199 CE. These findings imply construction dates around 180 CE for Makimuku Ishizuka and approximately 200 CE for Makimuku Katsuyama.<ref name=":03" />
== Dendrochronology == {| class="wikitable" !Dates CE !Ceramic types !Associated Kofun |- |158-188 |Makimuku 1 Shōnai 0 |Ishizuka |- |188-218 |Makimuku 2 Shōnai 1 |Katsuyama |- |218-243 |Makimuku 3 Shōnai 2/3 |Furu 0 |- |243-268 |Makimuku 4 Shōnai 3/5 |Furu 1 Hashihaka |}

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Completion tier 2]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]][[en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese tomb}} {{Draft topics|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|geo}}
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| image=File:Makimuku_kohungun_aerial.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E5%8B%9D%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3&params=34_32_50.7_N_135_50_5.2_E_type:landmark_region:JP}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]<ref name=":0">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>.It is a Kofun of [[Makimuku ruins]], and considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later.<ref name=":1">{{Cite book |last=Ki11er |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}</ref>{{rp|249}}
The [[Makimuku ruins]], have been very important in providing information for new chronologies of Ancient Japan. [[Hashihaka Kofun]] is the most famous member of the ruins. Two significant Kofuns from this region, namely the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Makimuku Ishizuka|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} and Makimuku Katsuyama, have provided dendrochronological evidence that suggests revisions to established ceramic chronologies.<ref name=":0" />
== Dating == The Kofun is dated to around 200CE, with the nearby {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} being dated to around 180AD.<ref name=":03">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>
It is considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later, so its chrronological position is notable.<ref name=":1" />{{rp|249}}
In the 1990s, a wooden board from the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} was dated to 177 CE with dendrochronology. In 2001, other wood remains from the {{ill|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|ja|纒向勝山古墳}} were dated between 103 and 199 CE. These findings imply construction dates around 180 CE for Makimuku Ishizuka and approximately 200 CE for Makimuku Katsuyama.<ref name=":03" />
== Dendrochronology == {| class="wikitable" !Dates CE !Ceramic types !Associated Kofun |- |158-188 |Makimuku 1 Shōnai 0 |Ishizuka |- |188-218 |Makimuku 2 Shōnai 1 |Katsuyama |- |218-243 |Makimuku 3 Shōnai 2/3 |Furu 0 |- |243-268 |Makimuku 4 Shōnai 3/5 |Furu 1 Hashihaka |}
==References== {{reflist}}
== Further reading == * [https://heritageofjapan.wordpress.com/yayoi-era-yields-up-rice/the-advent-of-agriculture-and-the-rice-revolution/who-was-queen-himiko/the-yamatai-puzzle-where-was-himikos-headquarters/in-the-news-hashihaka-tomb-may-be-queen-himikos/in-the-news-hokenoyama-tomb-in-nara-queen-himikos-tomb/]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Completion tier 1]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]][[en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese tomb}} {{Draft topics|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|geo}}
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| image=File:Makimuku_kohungun_aerial.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E5%8B%9D%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3&params=34_32_50.7_N_135_50_5.2_E_type:landmark_region:JP}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]<ref name=":0">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>.It is a Kofun of [[Makimuku ruins]], and considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later.<ref name=":1">{{Cite book |last=Ki11er |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}</ref>{{rp|249}}
The [[Makimuku ruins]], have been very important in providing information for new chronologies of Ancient Japan. [[Hashihaka Kofun]] is the most famous member of the ruins. Two significant Kofuns from this region, namely the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Makimuku Ishizuka|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} and Makimuku Katsuyama, have provided dendrochronological evidence that suggests revisions to established ceramic chronologies.<ref name=":0" />
== Dating == The Kofun is dated to around 200CE, with the nearby {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} being dated to around 180AD.<ref name=":03">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>
It is considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later, so its chrronological position is notable.<ref name=":1" />{{rp|249}}
In the 1990s, a wooden board from the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} was dated to 177 CE with dendrochronology. In 2001, other wood remains from the {{ill|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|ja|纒向勝山古墳}} were dated between 103 and 199 CE. These findings imply construction dates around 180 CE for Makimuku Ishizuka and approximately 200 CE for Makimuku Katsuyama.<ref name=":03" />
== Dendrochronology == {| class="wikitable" !Dates CE !Ceramic types !Associated Kofun |- |158-188 |Makimuku 1 Shōnai 0 |Ishizuka |- |188-218 |Makimuku 2 Shōnai 1 |Katsuyama |- |218-243 |Makimuku 3 Shōnai 2/3 |Furu 0 |- |243-268 |Makimuku 4 Shōnai 3/5 |Furu 1 Hashihaka |}
==References== {{reflist}}
== Further reading == * [https://heritageofjapan.wordpress.com/yayoi-era-yields-up-rice/the-advent-of-agriculture-and-the-rice-revolution/who-was-queen-himiko/the-yamatai-puzzle-where-was-himikos-headquarters/in-the-news-hashihaka-tomb-may-be-queen-himikos/in-the-news-hokenoyama-tomb-in-nara-queen-himikos-tomb/]

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]][[en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese tomb}} {{Draft topics|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|geo}}
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| image=File:Makimuku_kohungun_aerial.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E5%8B%9D%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3&params=34_32_50.7_N_135_50_5.2_E_type:landmark_region:JP}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]<ref name=":0">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>.It is a Kofun of [[Makimuku ruins]], and considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later.<ref name=":1">{{Cite book |last=Ki11er |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}</ref>{{rp|249}}
The [[Makimuku ruins]], have been very important in providing information for new chronologies of Ancient Japan. [[Hashihaka Kofun]] is the most famous member of the ruins. Two significant Kofuns from this region, namely the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Makimuku Ishizuka|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} and Makimuku Katsuyama, have provided dendrochronological evidence that suggests revisions to established ceramic chronologies.<ref name=":0" />
== Dating == The Kofun is dated to around 200CE, with the nearby {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} being dated to around 180AD.<ref name=":03">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>
It is considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later, so its chrronological position is notable.<ref name=":1" />{{rp|249}}
In the 1990s, a wooden board from the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} was dated to 177 CE with dendrochronology. In 2001, other wood remains from the {{ill|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|ja|纒向勝山古墳}} were dated between 103 and 199 CE. These findings imply construction dates around 180 CE for Makimuku Ishizuka and approximately 200 CE for Makimuku Katsuyama.<ref name=":03" />
== Dendrochronology == {| class="wikitable" !Dates CE !Ceramic types !Associated Kofun |- |158-188 |Makimuku 1 Shōnai 0 |Ishizuka |- |188-218 |Makimuku 2 Shōnai 1 |Katsuyama |- |218-243 |Makimuku 3 Shōnai 2/3 |Furu 0 |- |243-268 |Makimuku 4 Shōnai 3/5 |Furu 1 Hashihaka |}

revision.comment: full transfer of all these templates down to the bottom. Makes [[June (Lunisolar)]] enwiki link work again
revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]][[en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese tomb}} {{Draft topics|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|geo}}
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| image=File:Makimuku_kohungun_aerial.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E5%8B%9D%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3&params=34_32_50.7_N_135_50_5.2_E_type:landmark_region:JP}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]<ref name=":0">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>.It is a Kofun of [[Makimuku ruins]], and considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later.<ref name=":1">{{Cite book |last=Ki11er |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}</ref>{{rp|249}}
The [[Makimuku ruins]], have been very important in providing information for new chronologies of Ancient Japan. [[Hashihaka Kofun]] is the most famous member of the ruins. Two significant Kofuns from this region, namely the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Makimuku Ishizuka|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} and Makimuku Katsuyama, have provided dendrochronological evidence that suggests revisions to established ceramic chronologies.<ref name=":0" />
== Dating == The Kofun is dated to around 200CE, with the nearby {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} being dated to around 180AD.<ref name=":03">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>
It is considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later, so its chrronological position is notable.<ref name=":1" />{{rp|249}}
In the 1990s, a wooden board from the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} was dated to 177 CE with dendrochronology. In 2001, other wood remains from the {{ill|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|ja|纒向勝山古墳}} were dated between 103 and 199 CE. These findings imply construction dates around 180 CE for Makimuku Ishizuka and approximately 200 CE for Makimuku Katsuyama.<ref name=":03" />
== Dendrochronology == {| class="wikitable" !Dates CE !Ceramic types !Associated Kofun |- |158-188 |Makimuku 1 Shōnai 0 |Ishizuka |- |188-218 |Makimuku 2 Shōnai 1 |Katsuyama |- |218-243 |Makimuku 3 Shōnai 2/3 |Furu 0 |- |243-268 |Makimuku 4 Shōnai 3/5 |Furu 1 Hashihaka |}
==References== {{reflist}}
== Further reading == * [https://heritageofjapan.wordpress.com/yayoi-era-yields-up-rice/the-advent-of-agriculture-and-the-rice-revolution/who-was-queen-himiko/the-yamatai-puzzle-where-was-himikos-headquarters/in-the-news-hashihaka-tomb-may-be-queen-himikos/in-the-news-hokenoyama-tomb-in-nara-queen-himikos-tomb/]

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]][[en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese tomb}} {{Draft topics|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|geo}}
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| image=File:Makimuku_kohungun_aerial.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E5%8B%9D%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3&params=34_32_50.7_N_135_50_5.2_E_type:landmark_region:JP}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]<ref name=":0">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>.It is a Kofun of [[Makimuku ruins]], and considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later.<ref name=":1">{{Cite book |last=Ki11er |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}</ref>{{rp|249}}
The [[Makimuku ruins]], have been very important in providing information for new chronologies of Ancient Japan. [[Hashihaka Kofun]] is the most famous member of the ruins. Two significant Kofuns from this region, namely the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Makimuku Ishizuka|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} and Makimuku Katsuyama, have provided dendrochronological evidence that suggests revisions to established ceramic chronologies.<ref name=":0" />
== Dating == The Kofun is dated to around 200CE, with the nearby {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} being dated to around 180AD.<ref name=":03">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>
It is considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later, so its chrronological position is notable.<ref name=":1" />{{rp|249}}
In the 1990s, a wooden board from the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} was dated to 177 CE with dendrochronology. In 2001, other wood remains from the {{ill|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|ja|纒向勝山古墳}} were dated between 103 and 199 CE. These findings imply construction dates around 180 CE for Makimuku Ishizuka and approximately 200 CE for Makimuku Katsuyama.<ref name=":03" />
== Dendrochronology == {| class="wikitable" !Dates CE !Ceramic types !Associated Kofun |- |158-188 |Makimuku 1 Shōnai 0 |Ishizuka |- |188-218 |Makimuku 2 Shōnai 1 |Katsuyama |- |218-243 |Makimuku 3 Shōnai 2/3 |Furu 0 |- |243-268 |Makimuku 4 Shōnai 3/5 |Furu 1 Hashihaka |}

Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]][[en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese tomb}} {{Draft topics|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|geo}}
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| image=File:Makimuku_kohungun_aerial.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E5%8B%9D%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3&params=34_32_50.7_N_135_50_5.2_E_type:landmark_region:JP}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]<ref name=":0">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>.It is a Kofun of [[Makimuku ruins]], and considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later.<ref name=":1">{{Cite book |last=Ki11er |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}</ref>{{rp|249}}
The [[Makimuku ruins]], have been very important in providing information for new chronologies of Ancient Japan. [[Hashihaka Kofun]] is the most famous member of the ruins. Two significant Kofuns from this region, namely the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Makimuku Ishizuka|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} and Makimuku Katsuyama, have provided dendrochronological evidence that suggests revisions to established ceramic chronologies.<ref name=":0" />
== Dating == The Kofun is dated to around 200CE, with the nearby {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} being dated to around 180AD.<ref name=":03">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>
It is considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later, so its chrronological position is notable.<ref name=":1" />{{rp|249}}
In the 1990s, a wooden board from the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} was dated to 177 CE with dendrochronology. In 2001, other wood remains from the {{ill|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|ja|纒向勝山古墳}} were dated between 103 and 199 CE. These findings imply construction dates around 180 CE for Makimuku Ishizuka and approximately 200 CE for Makimuku Katsuyama.<ref name=":03" />
== Dendrochronology == {| class="wikitable" !Dates CE !Ceramic types !Associated Kofun |- |158-188 |Makimuku 1 Shōnai 0 |Ishizuka |- |188-218 |Makimuku 2 Shōnai 1 |Katsuyama |- |218-243 |Makimuku 3 Shōnai 2/3 |Furu 0 |- |243-268 |Makimuku 4 Shōnai 3/5 |Furu 1 Hashihaka |}

revision.comment: moving the kofun template to the bottom of each page
revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]][[en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese tomb}} {{Draft topics|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|geo}}
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| image=File:Makimuku_kohungun_aerial.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E5%8B%9D%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3&params=34_32_50.7_N_135_50_5.2_E_type:landmark_region:JP}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]<ref name=":0">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>.It is a Kofun of [[Makimuku ruins]], and considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later.<ref name=":1">{{Cite book |last=Ki11er |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}</ref>{{rp|249}}
The [[Makimuku ruins]], have been very important in providing information for new chronologies of Ancient Japan. [[Hashihaka Kofun]] is the most famous member of the ruins. Two significant Kofuns from this region, namely the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Makimuku Ishizuka|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} and Makimuku Katsuyama, have provided dendrochronological evidence that suggests revisions to established ceramic chronologies.<ref name=":0" />
== Dating == The Kofun is dated to around 200CE, with the nearby {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} being dated to around 180AD.<ref name=":03">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>
It is considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later, so its chrronological position is notable.<ref name=":1" />{{rp|249}}
In the 1990s, a wooden board from the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} was dated to 177 CE with dendrochronology. In 2001, other wood remains from the {{ill|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|ja|纒向勝山古墳}} were dated between 103 and 199 CE. These findings imply construction dates around 180 CE for Makimuku Ishizuka and approximately 200 CE for Makimuku Katsuyama.<ref name=":03" />
== Dendrochronology == {| class="wikitable" !Dates CE !Ceramic types !Associated Kofun |- |158-188 |Makimuku 1 Shōnai 0 |Ishizuka |- |188-218 |Makimuku 2 Shōnai 1 |Katsuyama |- |218-243 |Makimuku 3 Shōnai 2/3 |Furu 0 |- |243-268 |Makimuku 4 Shōnai 3/5 |Furu 1 Hashihaka |}
==References== {{reflist}}

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| image=File:Makimuku_kohungun_aerial.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E5%8B%9D%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3&params=34_32_50.7_N_135_50_5.2_E_type:landmark_region:JP}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]<ref name=":0">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>.It is a Kofun of [[Makimuku ruins]], and considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later.<ref name=":1">{{Cite book |last=Ki11er |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}</ref>{{rp|249}}
The [[Makimuku ruins]], have been very important in providing information for new chronologies of Ancient Japan. [[Hashihaka Kofun]] is the most famous member of the ruins. Two significant Kofuns from this region, namely the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Makimuku Ishizuka|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} and Makimuku Katsuyama, have provided dendrochronological evidence that suggests revisions to established ceramic chronologies.<ref name=":0" />
== Dating == The Kofun is dated to around 200CE, with the nearby {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} being dated to around 180AD.<ref name=":03">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>
It is considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later, so its chrronological position is notable.<ref name=":1" />{{rp|249}}
In the 1990s, a wooden board from the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} was dated to 177 CE with dendrochronology. In 2001, other wood remains from the {{ill|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|ja|纒向勝山古墳}} were dated between 103 and 199 CE. These findings imply construction dates around 180 CE for Makimuku Ishizuka and approximately 200 CE for Makimuku Katsuyama.<ref name=":03" />
== Dendrochronology == {| class="wikitable" !Dates CE !Ceramic types !Associated Kofun |- |158-188 |Makimuku 1 Shōnai 0 |Ishizuka |- |188-218 |Makimuku 2 Shōnai 1 |Katsuyama |- |218-243 |Makimuku 3 Shōnai 2/3 |Furu 0 |- |243-268 |Makimuku 4 Shōnai 3/5 |Furu 1 Hashihaka |}
==References== {{reflist}}

[[Category:New]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} [[Category:completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] {{Kofun navbar}}

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]][[en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese tomb}} {{Draft topics|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|geo}}
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| image=File:Makimuku_kohungun_aerial.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E5%8B%9D%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3&params=34_32_50.7_N_135_50_5.2_E_type:landmark_region:JP}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]<ref name=":0">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>.It is a Kofun of [[Makimuku ruins]], and considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later.<ref name=":1">{{Cite book |last=Ki11er |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}</ref>{{rp|249}}
The [[Makimuku ruins]], have been very important in providing information for new chronologies of Ancient Japan. [[Hashihaka Kofun]] is the most famous member of the ruins. Two significant Kofuns from this region, namely the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Makimuku Ishizuka|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} and Makimuku Katsuyama, have provided dendrochronological evidence that suggests revisions to established ceramic chronologies.<ref name=":0" />
== Dating == The Kofun is dated to around 200CE, with the nearby {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} being dated to around 180AD.<ref name=":03">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>
It is considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later, so its chrronological position is notable.<ref name=":1" />{{rp|249}}
In the 1990s, a wooden board from the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} was dated to 177 CE with dendrochronology. In 2001, other wood remains from the {{ill|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|ja|纒向勝山古墳}} were dated between 103 and 199 CE. These findings imply construction dates around 180 CE for Makimuku Ishizuka and approximately 200 CE for Makimuku Katsuyama.<ref name=":03" />
== Dendrochronology == {| class="wikitable" !Dates CE !Ceramic types !Associated Kofun |- |158-188 |Makimuku 1 Shōnai 0 |Ishizuka |- |188-218 |Makimuku 2 Shōnai 1 |Katsuyama |- |218-243 |Makimuku 3 Shōnai 2/3 |Furu 0 |- |243-268 |Makimuku 4 Shōnai 3/5 |Furu 1 Hashihaka |}
==References== {{reflist}}

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]][[en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese tomb}} {{Draft topics|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|geo}}
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| image=File:Makimuku_kohungun_aerial.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E5%8B%9D%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3&params=34_32_50.7_N_135_50_5.2_E_type:landmark_region:JP}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]<ref name=":0">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>.It is a Kofun of [[Makimuku ruins]], and considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later.<ref name=":1">{{Cite book |last=Ki11er |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}</ref>{{rp|249}}
The [[Makimuku ruins]], have been very important in providing information for new chronologies of Ancient Japan. [[Hashihaka Kofun]] is the most famous member of the ruins. Two significant Kofuns from this region, namely the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Makimuku Ishizuka|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} and Makimuku Katsuyama, have provided dendrochronological evidence that suggests revisions to established ceramic chronologies.<ref name=":0" />
== Dating == The Kofun is dated to around 200CE, with the nearby {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} being dated to around 180AD.<ref name=":03">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>
It is considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later, so its chrronological position is notable.<ref name=":1" />{{rp|249}}
In the 1990s, a wooden board from the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} was dated to 177 CE with dendrochronology. In 2001, other wood remains from the {{ill|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|ja|纒向勝山古墳}} were dated between 103 and 199 CE. These findings imply construction dates around 180 CE for Makimuku Ishizuka and approximately 200 CE for Makimuku Katsuyama.<ref name=":03" />
== Dendrochronology == {| class="wikitable" !Dates CE !Ceramic types !Associated Kofun |- |158-188 |Makimuku 1 Shōnai 0 |Ishizuka |- |188-218 |Makimuku 2 Shōnai 1 |Katsuyama |- |218-243 |Makimuku 3 Shōnai 2/3 |Furu 0 |- |243-268 |Makimuku 4 Shōnai 3/5 |Furu 1 Hashihaka |}

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]][[en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese tomb}} {{Draft topics|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|geo}}
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| image=File:Makimuku_kohungun_aerial.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E5%8B%9D%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3&params=34_32_50.7_N_135_50_5.2_E_type:landmark_region:JP}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]<ref name=":0">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>.It is a Kofun of [[Makimuku ruins]], and considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later.<ref name=":1">{{Cite book |last=Ki11er |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}</ref>{{rp|249}}
The [[Makimuku ruins]], have been very important in providing information for new chronologies of Ancient Japan. [[Hashihaka Kofun]] is the most famous member of the ruins. Two significant Kofuns from this region, namely the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Makimuku Ishizuka|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} and Makimuku Katsuyama, have provided dendrochronological evidence that suggests revisions to established ceramic chronologies.<ref name=":0" />
== Dating == The Kofun is dated to around 200CE, with the nearby {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} being dated to around 180AD.<ref name=":03">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>
It is considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later, so its chrronological position is notable.<ref name=":1" />{{rp|249}}
In the 1990s, a wooden board from the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} was dated to 177 CE with dendrochronology. In 2001, other wood remains from the {{ill|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|ja|纒向勝山古墳}} were dated between 103 and 199 CE. These findings imply construction dates around 180 CE for Makimuku Ishizuka and approximately 200 CE for Makimuku Katsuyama.<ref name=":03" />
== Dendrochronology == {| class="wikitable" !Dates CE !Ceramic types !Associated Kofun |- |158-188 |Makimuku 1 Shōnai 0 |Ishizuka |- |188-218 |Makimuku 2 Shōnai 1 |Katsuyama |- |218-243 |Makimuku 3 Shōnai 2/3 |Furu 0 |- |243-268 |Makimuku 4 Shōnai 3/5 |Furu 1 Hashihaka |}

Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]][[en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese tomb}} {{Draft topics|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|geo}}
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| image=File:Makimuku_kohungun_aerial.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E5%8B%9D%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3&params=34_32_50.7_N_135_50_5.2_E_type:landmark_region:JP}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]<ref name=":0">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>.It is a Kofun of [[Makimuku ruins]], and considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later.<ref name=":1">{{Cite book |last=Ki11er |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}</ref>{{rp|249}}
The [[Makimuku ruins]], have been very important in providing information for new chronologies of Ancient Japan. [[Hashihaka Kofun]] is the most famous member of the ruins. Two significant Kofuns from this region, namely the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Makimuku Ishizuka|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} and Makimuku Katsuyama, have provided dendrochronological evidence that suggests revisions to established ceramic chronologies.<ref name=":0" />
== Dating == The Kofun is dated to around 200CE, with the nearby {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} being dated to around 180AD.<ref name=":03">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>
It is considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later, so its chrronological position is notable.<ref name=":1" />{{rp|249}}
In the 1990s, a wooden board from the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} was dated to 177 CE with dendrochronology. In 2001, other wood remains from the {{ill|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|ja|纒向勝山古墳}} were dated between 103 and 199 CE. These findings imply construction dates around 180 CE for Makimuku Ishizuka and approximately 200 CE for Makimuku Katsuyama.<ref name=":03" />
== Dendrochronology == {| class="wikitable" !Dates CE !Ceramic types !Associated Kofun |- |158-188 |Makimuku 1 Shōnai 0 |Ishizuka |- |188-218 |Makimuku 2 Shōnai 1 |Katsuyama |- |218-243 |Makimuku 3 Shōnai 2/3 |Furu 0 |- |243-268 |Makimuku 4 Shōnai 3/5 |Furu 1 Hashihaka |}

It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese tomb}} {{Draft topics|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|geo}}
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| image=File:Makimuku_kohungun_aerial.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E5%8B%9D%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3&params=34_32_50.7_N_135_50_5.2_E_type:landmark_region:JP}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]<ref name=":0">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>.It is a Kofun of [[Makimuku ruins]], and considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later.<ref name=":1">{{Cite book |last=Ki11er |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}</ref>{{rp|249}}
The [[Makimuku ruins]], have been very important in providing information for new chronologies of Ancient Japan. [[Hashihaka Kofun]] is the most famous member of the ruins. Two significant Kofuns from this region, namely the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Makimuku Ishizuka|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} and Makimuku Katsuyama, have provided dendrochronological evidence that suggests revisions to established ceramic chronologies.<ref name=":0" />
== Dating == The Kofun is dated to around 200CE, with the nearby {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} being dated to around 180AD.<ref name=":03">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>
It is considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later, so its chrronological position is notable.<ref name=":1" />{{rp|249}}
In the 1990s, a wooden board from the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} was dated to 177 CE with dendrochronology. In 2001, other wood remains from the {{ill|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|ja|纒向勝山古墳}} were dated between 103 and 199 CE. These findings imply construction dates around 180 CE for Makimuku Ishizuka and approximately 200 CE for Makimuku Katsuyama.<ref name=":03" />
== Dendrochronology == {| class="wikitable" !Dates CE !Ceramic types !Associated Kofun |- |158-188 |Makimuku 1 Shōnai 0 |Ishizuka |- |188-218 |Makimuku 2 Shōnai 1 |Katsuyama |- |218-243 |Makimuku 3 Shōnai 2/3 |Furu 0 |- |243-268 |Makimuku 4 Shōnai 3/5 |Furu 1 Hashihaka |}
==References== {{reflist}}

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]][[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]][[Category:qq]] on (CatAlot)
revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]][[en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese tomb}} {{Draft topics|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|geo}}
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| image=File:Makimuku_kohungun_aerial.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E5%8B%9D%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3&params=34_32_50.7_N_135_50_5.2_E_type:landmark_region:JP}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a [[Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]<ref name=":0">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>.It is a Kofun of [[Makimuku ruins]], and considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later.<ref name=":1">{{Cite book |last=Ki11er |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}</ref>{{rp|249}}
The [[Makimuku ruins]], have been very important in providing information for new chronologies of Ancient Japan. [[Hashihaka Kofun]] is the most famous member of the ruins. Two significant Kofuns from this region, namely the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Makimuku Ishizuka|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} and Makimuku Katsuyama, have provided dendrochronological evidence that suggests revisions to established ceramic chronologies.<ref name=":0" />
== Dating == The Kofun is dated to around 200CE, with the nearby {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} being dated to around 180AD.<ref name=":03">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>
It is considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later, so its chrronological position is notable.<ref name=":1" />{{rp|249}}
In the 1990s, a wooden board from the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} was dated to 177 CE with dendrochronology. In 2001, other wood remains from the {{ill|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|ja|纒向勝山古墳}} were dated between 103 and 199 CE. These findings imply construction dates around 180 CE for Makimuku Ishizuka and approximately 200 CE for Makimuku Katsuyama.<ref name=":03" />
== Dendrochronology == {| class="wikitable" !Dates CE !Ceramic types !Associated Kofun |- |158-188 |Makimuku 1 Shōnai 0 |Ishizuka |- |188-218 |Makimuku 2 Shōnai 1 |Katsuyama |- |218-243 |Makimuku 3 Shōnai 2/3 |Furu 0 |- |243-268 |Makimuku 4 Shōnai 3/5 |Furu 1 Hashihaka |}
==References== {{reflist}}
== Further reading == * [https://heritageofjapan.wordpress.com/yayoi-era-yields-up-rice/the-advent-of-agriculture-and-the-rice-revolution/who-was-queen-himiko/the-yamatai-puzzle-where-was-himikos-headquarters/in-the-news-hashihaka-tomb-may-be-queen-himikos/in-the-news-hokenoyama-tomb-in-nara-queen-himikos-tomb/]

revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese tomb}} {{Draft topics|east-asia}} {{AfC topic|geo}}
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| image=File:Makimuku_kohungun_aerial.jpg|coordinates=https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?language=ja&pagename=%E7%BA%92%E5%90%91%E5%8B%9D%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3&params=34_32_50.7_N_135_50_5.2_E_type:landmark_region:JP}} [[Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun]] is a [[Kofun]] found on the premises of [[Makimuku ruins]]<ref name=":0">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>.It is a Kofun of [[Makimuku ruins]], and considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later.<ref name=":1">{{Cite book |last=Ki11er |first=J. Edward |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848 |title=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |date=2017-12-18 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}</ref>{{rp|249}}
The [[Makimuku ruins]], have been very important in providing information for new chronologies of Ancient Japan. [[Hashihaka Kofun]] is the most famous member of the ruins. Two significant Kofuns from this region, namely the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|lt=Makimuku Ishizuka|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} and Makimuku Katsuyama, have provided dendrochronological evidence that suggests revisions to established ceramic chronologies.<ref name=":0" />
== Dating == The Kofun is dated to around 200CE, with the nearby {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} being dated to around 180AD.<ref name=":03">https://seaa-web.org/sites/default/files/publications/bseaa-1/BSEAA1-Rew-Styles.pdf</ref>
It is considered a transitional Kofun from the {{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-type kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} of earlier times to the [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole shaped Kofun]] that would dominate later, so its chrronological position is notable.<ref name=":1" />{{rp|249}}
In the 1990s, a wooden board from the {{ill|Makimuku Ishizuka Kofun|ja|纒向石塚古墳}} was dated to 177 CE with dendrochronology. In 2001, other wood remains from the {{ill|Makimuku Katsuyama Kofun|ja|纒向勝山古墳}} were dated between 103 and 199 CE. These findings imply construction dates around 180 CE for Makimuku Ishizuka and approximately 200 CE for Makimuku Katsuyama.<ref name=":03" />
== Dendrochronology == {| class="wikitable" !Dates CE !Ceramic types !Associated Kofun |- |158-188 |Makimuku 1 Shōnai 0 |Ishizuka |- |188-218 |Makimuku 2 Shōnai 1 |Katsuyama |- |218-243 |Makimuku 3 Shōnai 2/3 |Furu 0 |- |243-268 |Makimuku 4 Shōnai 3/5 |Furu 1 Hashihaka |}
==References== {{reflist}}
== Further reading == * [https://heritageofjapan.wordpress.com/yayoi-era-yields-up-rice/the-advent-of-agriculture-and-the-rice-revolution/who-was-queen-himiko/the-yamatai-puzzle-where-was-himikos-headquarters/in-the-news-hashihaka-tomb-may-be-queen-himikos/in-the-news-hokenoyama-tomb-in-nara-queen-himikos-tomb/]
